learning_outcomes.description: Working both individually and collaboratively to present a novel analysis of the nature of innovation.
Critically evaluate the nature and context of the innovation process and how it is changing.
Apply innovative frameworks and theories to create and communicate insights into innovation strategy for an organisation.
Develop an innovation strategy for an organisation.

assessments.due_date:
Week 3

This will be due in class beginning in Week 3
assessments.task_description:
This is an interactive oral assessment (IOA) based on a panel discussion. A small number of students will be randomly asked at the start of class to participate in the assessed panel discussion. This discussion will then lay the foundation for collaborative work focused on that session’s learning outcomes.

The IOA will be a structured, face-to-face evaluation involving open-ended questions as well as follow-up clarifying questions. Think of it like the Q&A session after a presentation. I’ll be asking you questions that link foundational concepts to practical applications each week. The goal is not to play “gotcha” but to provide you with a platform to demonstrate your learning.

You will be assessed on the clarity, depth, and accuracy of your responses, as well as your ability to logically connect insights to evidence.

We will begin the semester by running practice sessions to get you comfortable with the format and receive feedback.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This is a team assignment. You will be assigned to teams of 3-5 people. The task for this assignment is to create an image-based essay describing an innovation. It will consist primarily of images (e.g. photos; graphs etc) presented to the class on the due date. You will have to upload your presentation to Blackboard before commencement of class. The image-based essay should be structured as follows:

Part 1: Select an innovation that a random person on the street would not be aware of (e.g. don't tell us about your iPhone!). Draw on course concepts to describe the type of innovation, what makes it innovative, and how and why it was developed.

Part 2: Identify and illustrate the "enabling technologies" that needed to be in place for this innovation to be created and successfully diffuse.

Part 3: Drawing on course concepts to identify the "winners" and "losers" in terms of who has captured (or not) the value associated with this innovation's diffusion.

Part 4: Drawing on course concepts, identify what you believe was the single most important innovation leadership challenge associated with this innovation's development and diffusion.

Ideally, I would like you to make as many of the images as possible yourself. However, if you cannot make an original image, you can source (or generate) images from other sources. I'm not too worried about how “polished” the images are, as long as I can tell what they are and how they fit into your “story”.

Please note, while not all group members are required to present, all are expected to contribute to the development of the presentation and be sufficiently familiar with its content to participate in discussion during and after the presentation.

The post-presentation Q&A serves as assessment assurance. Students who are unable to demonstrate a reasonable understanding of the work submitted by their group may have their individual mark reduced.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The assignment is to write a 1500-word reflective essay based on your experience with innovation within an industry. You will draw on your knowledge of innovation to identify a technological change that is (or might be) fundamentally transforming the nature of an industry you are interested in. Salient trends could range from the artificial insemination of animals through to the shift to "green steel" production and onwards to AI-generated video for advertising. Reflecting on your experience and knowledge of this change:

  1. Use concepts from the course to describe the change and how it did (or might) impact existing business models within the industry
  2. Identify the single most important innovation management problem you believe this trend posed (or is posing) to your industry, justify why this is so, and how one firm impacted should respond differently.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You will work as a team to evaluate the innovation strategy of an agreed case (e.g. your current or a real life case I provide) using appropriate innovation frameworks to help you diagnose the state of play (e.g. innovation value chain; profiting from innovation etc) and other relevant analytical tools that have been discussed in this course.

Specifically you are to:

  • Conduct an assessment of the current state of innovation strategy within the organisation.
  • Make recommendations for improvement. The analysis and recommendations should be derived using the innovation tools concepts frameworks discussed in this course.

You will be asked to submit a 1 page overview that identifies your focal organisation at an agreed upon point, a brief description of the chosen organisation and the intended main objectives and/or expected outcomes of your planned study. This will not be formally graded, but this will instead serve as an opportunity to gain feedback. It is imperative that you complete this step to ensure that your study organisation selection and intended approach align with expectations.

The final report should be tightly written for an executive audience max 3000 words.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
